content.text:
DeepSeek (including V3, V3.2, and R1 variants) and Kimi AI (especially K2 and K2.5) are dominating recent English-language discussions on X as top-tier, cost-effective Chinese AI models challenging Western leaders like Claude, GPT, and Qwen in coding, reasoning, finance, vision, and agentic tasks.[[1]](https://x.com/i/status/2019199266879905950)[[2]](https://x.com/i/status/2018172906405282274)[[3]](https://x.com/i/status/2019023398912012765)

### Key Highlights from Recent Posts (Feb 2026):
- **Benchmarks & Performance**: Kimi K2.5 (Thinking) leads in corporate finance tasks, edging out Qwen3-Max-Thinking, with DeepSeek V3.2 praised for reasoning and long-context handling. Users recommend combos like GLM-4.7/MiniMax M2.1/Kimi K2.5 for coding, DeepSeek V3.2/Kimi K2 for writing/general queries.[[1]](https://x.com/i/status/2019199266879905950)[[4]](https://x.com/i/status/2019198806521237987)[[2]](https://x.com/i/status/2018172906405282274)
- **Availability & Free Access**: DeepSeek-V3.2 (685B params) now on Nebius Token Factory alongside others; Kimi K2.5 free via Nvidia's build platform or OpenRouter (non-quantized via Parasail for best quality). DeepSeek often listed as a top free ChatGPT alternative for research/coding.[[4]](https://x.com/i/status/2019198806521237987)[[5]](https://x.com/i/status/2018710065239269785)[[6]](https://x.com/i/status/2019057952414659038)
- **Tech Innovations**: DeepSeek-OCR 2 uses "causal flow tokens" to reorder image patches like human foveal vision for better OCR/multimodal. Kimi K2.5 built on DeepSeek V3 base, excelling in vision-to-code, agents, and deployment.[[7]](https://x.com/i/status/2018595653027549690)[[8]](https://x.com/i/status/2017642578855006307)
- **User Sentiment**: "2026 is the year of open-source AI" with these closing the gap on closed models; ideal startup stacks (e.g., Kimi K2 for writer/dev, DeepSeek R1 for researcher). Some note quantization issues hurting non-official providers.[[2]](https://x.com/i/status/2018172906405282274)[[3]](https://x.com/i/status/2019023398912012765)[[9]](https://x.com/i/status/2017526637101768910)

Overall, buzz positions them as "cheat codes" for devs—cheap, powerful, and rapidly improving, with Kimi K2.5 frequently called out for execution edge over pure coders like DeepSeek.[[10]](https://x.com/i/status/2019197711107711106) For hands-on, check deepseek.com or Moonshot AI's Kimi via free tiers.[[11]](https://x.com/i/status/2017817726602121403)
